seoTitle: Lockridge vs Lol Gray — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Lockridge and Lol Gray. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Lockridge vs Lol Gray: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Lockridge and Lol Gray.
heading: Lockridge vs Lol Gray — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Lockridge, Western Australia and Lol Gray, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Lockridge has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Lol Gray (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Lockridge or Lol Gray safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Lockridge has a flood risk score of 57/100, while Lol Gray scores 47/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Lockridge scores 30/100 for bushfire risk. Lol Gray scores 46/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Lockridge or Lol Gray?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Lockridge has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.48% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
